channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Argentina's Milei skirts Congress to seat a divisive judge in the Supreme Court
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — Argentina's President Javier Milei temporarily appointed two Supreme Court judges by decree Tuesday, bypassing Congress during its summer recess to push through a particularly controversial candidate in a move criticized as an overreach of executive power.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Botswana, the country with the biggest diamonds, gets an improved 10-year deal with miner De Beers
item.description: GABORONE, Botswana (AP) — The Botswana government signed a new diamond sales agreement crucial to its economy with the De Beers mining company on Tuesday after seven years of negotiations.
item.dc:creator: SELLO MOTSETA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Power outage in Chile leaves millions without electricity as officials scramble for answers
item.description: A widespread power outage in the South American nation of Chile left millions of people without electricity Tuesday, according to news reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis in critical but stable condition, has CT scan, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis remains in critical condition but is stable and returned to work Tuesday evening, the Vatican announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In Rome, talks to protect Earth's biodiversity resume with money topping the agenda
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — An annual United Nations conference on biodiversity that ran out of time last year will resume its work Tuesday in Rome with money at the top of the agenda.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trapped in Gaza: Residents speak out as Egypt blocks exit, rejects Trump’s relocation plan
item.description: Gazans trapped by closed borders voice frustration, with some supporting Trump’s relocation plan as Egypt blocks exits and Hamas clings to control amid devastation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trial begins in Germany for 4 alleged Hamas members accused of seeking weapons caches across Europe
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A trial began Tuesday in Germany for four alleged Hamas members suspected of organizing weapons caches across Europe. The country's top prosecutor accuses the men of membership in a foreign terrorist organization. It’s a pilot case for prosecutors, German news agency dpa reported.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK to raise defense spending to 2.5% of GDP by 2027, Starmer says before Trump meeting
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer says the U.K. will raise defense spending to 2.5% of gross domestic product by 2027.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Putin says Russia is open to economic cooperation with US on rare earth minerals
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said that Moscow and Washington could make a deal on mining rare earth minerals both in Russia and parts of Russian-occupied Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises: Vatican
item.description: “In the morning, after receiving the Eucharist, he resumed work activities,” the Vatican statement said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Son of Chinese journalist jailed for espionage calls for his father's release
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The son of a Chinese journalist accused of espionage called for his father's release from a seven-year prison sentence in the high-profile case that signaled Beijing's tighten grip on journalism.
item.dc:creator: FU TING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French surgeon accused of raping or abusing 299 people, mostly child patients
item.description: VANNES, France (AP) — A former surgeon went on trial in France on Monday for the alleged rape or sexual abuse of 299 victims, most of them children who were his patients, in what investigators and his own notebooks describe as a pattern of violence spanning over three decades.
item.dc:creator: JADE LE DELEY and NICOLAS VAUX-MONTAGNY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis shows slight improvement and resumes some work, while still critical, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Monday but showed slight improvement in laboratory tests and resumed some work, the Vatican said, including calling a parish in Gaza City that he has kept in touch with since the war there began.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Merz prioritizes Ukraine and US, vows to tackle migration and economy after German election win
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Germany's mainstream conservatives led by Friedrich Merz won the country's national election, ensuring that Ukraine has an even stronger supporter in the European Union's largest country and creating hopes for renewal in an economic powerhouse that has been badly battered in recent yea
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Western leaders visit Kyiv and pledge military support against Russia on the war's 3rd anniversary
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Monday marked the bleakest anniversary yet of its war against the Russia invasion, with the country's forces under severe pressure on the battlefield and U.S. President Donald Trump's administration apparently embracing the Kremlin in a reversal of American policy.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: American detained in Paris after baby is allegedly thrown from hotel window
item.description: The American was taken to a hospital for a post-delivery operation where she was placed in custody, according to the Paris Prosecutor’s Office.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Nearly 200K sign petition to revoke Musk's Canadian citizenship
item.description: Thousands of individuals have signed an online petition urging the revocation of tech billionaire Elon Musk's Canadian citizenship over his involvement in the Trump administration, which the petitioners argue undermines Canada's sovereignty.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Pope shows slight improvement, resumes some work: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis is showing a “slight improvement” in his condition and has resumed some of his work activities, according to the Vatican’s Monday evening bulletin. The pope remains in critical condition, as he undergoes treatment for pneumonia in both lungs.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Pope Francis' health shows 'slight improvement' as he has 'resumed work,' Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis' health showed a “slight improvement" Monday as he “resumed work" at the hospital and called a priest at a parish in Gaza, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More pressure on Rwanda as Congo says rebel uprising has killed over 7,000 people this year
item.description: KINSHASA, Congo (AP) — More than 7,000 people have died this year as Rwanda-backed M23 rebels have captured unprecedented amounts of territory in mineral-rich eastern Congo, Congo's prime minister said Monday, as the European Union announced it would review an agreement with Rwanda on critical raw m
item.dc:creator: JEAN-YVES KAMALE and WILSON MCMAKIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Surgeon on trial for allegedly raping, abusing 299 victims, mostly child patients
item.description: A former French surgeon accused of raping or sexually abusing 299 victims, mostly children under his care, is set to stand trial in France on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: On third anniversary of Ukraine invasion, European leaders show support, express unease
item.description: European leaders voiced support for Ukraine on the third anniversary of the Russia invasion while demanding a say in any US-Russia peace arrangement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The biggest takeaways from Germany's election, which will bring change to the EU's leading power
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Germany faces its second change of leader in fewer than four years after the head of the center-right opposition, Friedrich Merz, won Sunday's election, which saw a surge for a far-right party and a stinging defeat for outgoing Chancellor Olaf Scholz.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis in critical condition with early kidney failure but remains alert as prayers pour in
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Sunday and blood tests showed early kidney failure but he remains alert, responsive and attended Mass, the Vatican said, as the 88-year-old pontiff battles pneumonia and a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Conservative opposition wins German election and the far right is 2nd with strongest postwar result
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — The opposition conservatives led by Friedrich Merz won a lackluster victory in Germany's election Sunday and Alternative for Germany doubled its support in the strongest showing for a far-right party since World War II, projections showed.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis suffering from 'mild renal insufficiency,' though condition remains 'under control,' Vatican says
item.description: Vatican officials announced on Sunday that Pope Francis is currently suffering from "mild renal insufficiency" amid his treatment for bilateral pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Delta faces first lawsuits days after plane flips in Toronto
item.description: All 21 people injured in the crash landing have been released from the hospital, and no one was killed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu decries release of wrong body as a ceasefire violation. Hamas pledges to investigate
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu vowed revenge Friday for what he described as a “cruel and malicious violation” of the ceasefire agreement after authorities determined that a body released by Hamas was not an Israeli mother of two small boys, as the militant group had prom
item.dc:creator: AREEJ HAZBOUN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Singer of Canadian anthem at 4 Nations Face-Off changes lyric to protest Trump's 51st state remarks
item.description: The anthem singer who performed the Canadian anthem prior to the 4 Nations Face-Off championship game Thursday night changed a lyric in “O Canada” as a response to U.S. President Donald Trump's repeated remarks about making the country the 51st state.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO and KEN POWTAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Medical oxygen in short supply in many parts of world: Research
item.description: "We found that more than 5 billion people—ie, more than 60% of the world's population—do not have access to safe, quality, and affordable medical oxygen services," the commission wrote.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation
